cert-baseline: All-GF(256) certified completion and the reference point of that arm: LDPC staircase plus 12 dense binary rows plus 12 periodic-Cauchy GF(256) heavy rows, mix-3 packets, staircase fanout N1=2 below K=10000 and 3 above.
cert-d16: Certified with the dense binary block grown from 12 to 16 rows; heavy band unchanged at 12.
cert-d16-h18: Certified with both blocks widened at once: 16 dense binary rows and 18 heavy GF(256) rows, the joint upper-right corner of the cert-d* / cert-h* sweeps.
cert-d24: Certified with 24 dense binary rows, the widest dense block screened; heavy band unchanged at 12.
cert-d8: Certified with the dense binary block cut from 12 to 8 rows; heavy band unchanged at 12.
cert-h18: Certified with the heavy band grown to 18 GF(256) rows.
cert-h24: Certified with the heavy band grown to 24 GF(256) rows, the widest heavy band screened.
cert-h6: Certified with the heavy band cut to 6 GF(256) rows: half the GF(256) arithmetic and half the guaranteed rank cover of cert-baseline.
cert-hashed: Certified baseline dimensions (12 dense, 12 heavy) but heavy coefficients drawn from a full-column hash family instead of the periodic Cauchy table, isolating periodicity artifacts in the heavy band.
cert-ic-h12: Round-2 speed probe: certified completion with the full 12-row heavy band plus the identity-corner dense block, so dense parities become encoder-computable by construction.
cert-ic-h8: Round-2 speed probe: identity-corner certified completion with the heavy band trimmed to 8 GF(256) rows, trading heavy-row rank cover for GF(256) throughput.
cert-mix2: Certified baseline with mix-2 repair packets: 2 precode columns per repair symbol instead of 3.
cert-sh2: Certified baseline with staircase source fanout pinned to N1=2 at every K. The certified rule already uses N1=2 below K=10000, so this differs from cert-baseline only in the two largest K regions, where the default is N1=3.
cert-sh4: Certified baseline with staircase source fanout raised to N1=4 at every K (default 2 below K=10000, 3 above), roughly doubling staircase edge count at small K.
dbstair-h15-p32: The same degree-balanced staircase applied to the H15 architecture (11+4 shared-X, P=32, mix-2, hashed seed 68 with an independently keyed extension schedule) rather than to the production point.
dbstair-p244: Degree-balanced staircase at the production point (mixed 10+2, P=244, frozen-X): a separately keyed greedy matching equalizes every staircase row's source degree to floor/ceil(K*N1/S), removing degree hot-spots at unchanged edge count.
h15-p32: H15 architecture at P=32: 11 GF(256) + 4 GF(2^16) rows on shared-X, mix-2 packets, a K-keyed hashed residue-bucket schedule (seed 68) and an independently keyed extension schedule (seed xor 78). It won the earlier tuned reliability campaigns; on this untuned screen it is mid-pack.
h15-p32-mix3: H15 at P=32 (11+4 shared-X, hashed seed 68 + independent extension schedule) reverted to mix-3 repair packets: isolates the packet mix count inside the H15 architecture, all else equal to h15-p32.
h15-p32-sharedext: H15 at P=32 (11+4 shared-X, mix-2, hashed seed 68) with the GF(2^16) rows sharing the GF(256) hashed schedule instead of their own keyed one (no seed-xor 78): isolates independent extension scheduling, all else equal to h15-p32.
h15-p48: H15 (11+4 shared-X, mix-2, hashed seed 68 + independent extension schedule) at P=48.
h15-p64: H15 (11+4 shared-X, mix-2, hashed seed 68 + independent extension schedule) at P=64.
h15-p96: H15 (11+4 shared-X, mix-2, hashed seed 68 + independent extension schedule) at P=96.
ic-11x4-p32: Identity corner on the widened completion band: 11 GF(256) + 4 GF(2^16) rows at P=32 shared-X, dense block left at 12 rows.
ic-9x2-p48x: Stacked speed probe: identity corner plus a trimmed 9+2 completion band at P=48 shared-X, dense block left at 12.
ic-d6-p244: Identity corner at P=244 frozen-X with only 6 dense rows, the thinnest identity-corner dense block screened.
ic-d8-9x2-p48x: Stacked speed probe: identity corner plus a 9+2 completion band plus an 8-row dense block at P=48 shared-X, every encode-side trim applied at once.
ic-d8-p244: Identity corner at P=244 frozen-X with the dense block trimmed to 8 rows.
ic-d8-p32x: Identity corner at P=32 shared-X with 8 dense rows: short period plus a trimmed dense block.
ic-d8-p48x: Identity corner at P=48 shared-X with 8 dense rows.
ic-mix2-p244: Identity corner at P=244 frozen-X combined with mix-2 repair packets: both encode-side savings stacked on the production geometry.
ic-p32x: Identity corner on P=32 shared-X with the full 12-row dense block.
identcorner-p244: Identity-corner dense block at the production point (mixed 10+2, P=244, frozen-X, 12 dense rows): each dense row spans the source and staircase columns plus exactly its own dense column, making the D2xD2 dense corner the identity, so dense parities are encoder-computable and independent by construction. Unavailable at K=2..5.
mask-p32-r7-prefix: The same 7-row grouped schedule at P=32 shared-X, but mask 0x3f8 selects the high rows Y=3..9 - a pure equation-row permutation of mask-p32-r7-suffix with identical arithmetic work.
mask-p32-r7-suffix: P=32 shared-X with 7 of the 10 fixed Cauchy-Y GF(256) rows moved onto a second full-cycle residue schedule C; mask 0x07f selects the low rows Y=0..6. The final H completion columns stay on schedule A so the encoder corner is byte-identical.
mask-p48-r3-prefix: The same 3-row grouped schedule at P=48 shared-X with mask 0x380 selecting the high rows Y=7..9.
mask-p48-r3-suffix: P=48 shared-X with only 3 Cauchy-Y GF(256) rows on the second residue schedule C; mask 0x007 selects Y=0..2.
mixed-p128-frozen: Frozen-X, P=128: longest power-of-two period screened, roughly half the production table.
mixed-p128-sharedx: P=128 with shared-X geometry: the GF(2^16) extension rows reuse the subfield rows' Cauchy X coordinates, which keeps the completion corner MDS even at short periods. Geometry twin of mixed-p128-frozen.
mixed-p16-frozen: Frozen-X, P=16: the shortest period screened (the floor is the H=12 heavy-row count). Coefficient tables shrink ~15x versus P=244, but only 16 distinct column residues exist, so short-period rank collisions are most likely here.
mixed-p24-frozen: Frozen-X, P=24: twice the period floor, second-shortest point of the frozen period sweep.
mixed-p24-sharedx: P=24 with shared-X geometry: the GF(2^16) extension rows reuse the subfield rows' Cauchy X coordinates, which keeps the completion corner MDS even at short periods. Geometry twin of mixed-p24-frozen.
mixed-p244-8x2: Round-2 row trim: 8+2 rows at P=244 frozen-X, two completion rows below production and the thinnest frozen completion band screened.
mixed-p244-9x2: Round-2 row trim: 9 GF(256) + 2 GF(2^16) rows at P=244 frozen-X, one completion row below production.
mixed-p244-baseline: Untuned reference point for the whole mixed arm: 10 GF(256) + 2 GF(2^16) completion rows, production coefficient period P=244, frozen power-X geometry, 12 dense binary rows, mix-3 repair packets. Every other mixed entry is a one-knob deviation from this.
mixed-p244-d16: Baseline P=244 frozen-X with the dense binary block grown from 12 to 16 rows: extra independent dense equations bought with extra encode and solve work.
mixed-p244-d4: Round-2 dense-knee probe: P=244 frozen-X with only 4 dense binary rows, the thinnest dense block screened. This is the configuration the v1 dispatch profile selects.
mixed-p244-d6: Round-2 dense-knee probe: P=244 frozen-X with only 6 dense binary rows, halving the production dense block.
mixed-p244-d8: Baseline P=244 frozen-X with the dense binary block cut from 12 to 8 rows: four fewer dense XOR rows to build and solve, four fewer independent dense equations.
mixed-p244-mix2: Baseline P=244 frozen-X with mix-2 repair packets: each repair symbol mixes 2 precode columns instead of 3, which is cheaper to encode but yields thinner, less independent equations.
mixed-p244-sharedx: Shared-X geometry at the full production period P=244: isolates the coefficient-geometry change alone, with every other knob equal to mixed-p244-baseline.
mixed-p31-12x4: The same 12+4 shared-X band at P=31 instead of 32: an odd period coprime to the power-of-two block cadence, testing whether the alignment rather than the size of P is what matters.
mixed-p32-10x4: 10 GF(256) + 4 GF(2^16) rows at P=32 on frozen-X: the production subfield band with the extension band doubled, and the only 4-extension-row point measured on frozen geometry.
mixed-p32-11x2: 11 GF(256) + 2 GF(2^16) completion rows at P=32 shared-X: one extra subfield row above the production 10+2 band.
mixed-p32-11x4: 11 GF(256) + 4 GF(2^16) completion rows at P=32 shared-X: the extension band is doubled on top of the widened subfield band, so extension-field work rises with the added rank cover.
mixed-p32-12x4: 12 GF(256) + 4 GF(2^16) rows at P=32 shared-X: the widest completion band screened (12 subfield rows require 4 extension rows).
mixed-p32-frozen: Frozen-X, P=32. This is the only period at which the production policy enables the joint A/B residue-bucket solver path, so it is the short-period candidate with a real implementation behind it.
mixed-p32-sharedx: P=32 with shared-X geometry: the GF(2^16) extension rows reuse the subfield rows' Cauchy X coordinates, which keeps the completion corner MDS even at short periods. Geometry twin of mixed-p32-frozen.
mixed-p32-sharedx-mix2: P=32 shared-X with mix-2 repair packets: the short-period, cheap-encode corner of the plain mixed grid.
mixed-p32x-d8: Round-2 dense-knee probe on the short-period shared-X arm: P=32 shared-X with 8 dense binary rows.
mixed-p48-frozen: Frozen-X, P=48: four times the period floor, the midpoint of the frozen period sweep.
mixed-p48-ramp: P=48 shared-X with the ramp residue schedule: the per-block residue advances through corner-safe boundary steps instead of staying constant.
mixed-p48-sharedx: P=48 with shared-X geometry: the GF(2^16) extension rows reuse the subfield rows' Cauchy X coordinates, which keeps the completion corner MDS even at short periods. Geometry twin of mixed-p48-frozen.
mixed-p48-skew16: P=48 shared-X with a block-residue skew of 16, twice the rotation of mixed-p48-skew8 (the corner-safe skew range at P=48 is 0..36).
mixed-p48-skew8: P=48 shared-X with a corner-preserving block-residue skew of 8: every complete period block is rotated by 8 residues, breaking the fixed column-to-residue alignment without disturbing the encoder corner.
mixed-p48x-8x2: Round-2 row trim on shared-X geometry: 8+2 completion rows at P=48.
mixed-p48x-9x2: Round-2 row trim on shared-X geometry: 9+2 completion rows at P=48.
mixed-p64-frozen: Frozen-X, P=64: double the P=32 short-period point, still a power of two.
mixed-p64-sharedx: P=64 with shared-X geometry: the GF(2^16) extension rows reuse the subfield rows' Cauchy X coordinates, which keeps the completion corner MDS even at short periods. Geometry twin of mixed-p64-frozen.
mixed-p96-11x2: 11+2 completion rows at P=96 shared-X; longest period of the 11+2 sweep.
mixed-p96-frozen: Frozen-X, P=96: the long end of the short-period frozen sweep, ~40% of the production period.
mixed-p96-ramp: Ramp residue schedule at P=96 shared-X: the longer-period twin of mixed-p48-ramp.
mixed-p96-sharedx: P=96 with shared-X geometry: the GF(2^16) extension rows reuse the subfield rows' Cauchy X coordinates, which keeps the completion corner MDS even at short periods. Geometry twin of mixed-p96-frozen.
seganchor-four0369: Segmented dense anchors at rows {0, 3, 6, 9}: four balanced restarts and three rows per segment, the most finely segmented dense block screened (P=244 frozen-X, otherwise baseline).
seganchor-three048: Segmented dense anchors at rows {0, 4, 8}: three balanced restarts split the 12-row dense block into three independently mixed 4-row segments (P=244 frozen-X, otherwise baseline).
twoanchor-adaptive-p244: The same two-anchor construction at P=244 frozen-X, but bound the way a named profile would ship it: the second anchor engages only from K>=4096, so every smaller K falls back to the plain single-anchor Shuffle-2 dense block.
twoanchor-mix2: Two-anchor dense block at P=244 frozen-X combined with mix-2 repair packets.
twoanchor-p244: Two-anchor dense block at the production point (P=244 frozen-X): rows 0-6 follow the usual Shuffle-2 flip chain, row 7 restarts from the balanced half of a second independently shuffled deck, rows 8-11 flip on from there. Always on, phase 0 - a second independent dense equation at zero extra rows.
twoanchor-p32x: Two-anchor dense block moved onto short-period P=32 shared-X geometry.
wh1-legacy: Original Wirehair codec: table-driven Soliton-style peel rows, a dense binary block whose size GROWS with K via the GetDenseCount(K) table, and a fixed 6x18 GF(256) Cauchy heavy band. Run here with uniform untuned seeds instead of its per-K production tables, so its 1.9-2.9% failures are the encoder matrix failing to construct at all, which no amount of received overhead can repair.
wh2-dispatched-v1: The selected production candidate: dispatch profile v1 maps a single band K=2..64000 onto mixed-p244-frozen-10x2-d4 (mixed 10+2 completion, P=244, frozen-X, 4 dense binary rows). The equation-preserving tiny-K fast path, auto-gated at K<=512 with block bytes<=8 and K*bytes<=3072, absorbed the former small-K regime, so no crossover and no second band survive; its recovery numbers are therefore identical to mixed-p244-d4.
